gist.headline: The golden handshake
gist.blurb: We've completely redesigned the Bring Your Own Pollen consent screen to be smoother, clearer, and a whole lot prettier. Connecting your account to new apps now feels less like signing a contract and more like a friendly high-five.
gist.summary: The BYOP authorization screen has been completely redesigned with a cleaner layout, upfront spending limits, and simplified model selection.
gist.impact: Users connecting their accounts to third-party apps will experience a much smoother, mobile-friendly approval flow with clearer controls over their pollen budget.
gist.visual_concept: The PR focuses on the BYOP (Bring Your Own Pollen) authorization screen redesign, specifically mentioning an 'amber' theme and app permissions. Key symbols: glowing amber screens, pollen/coins, approval stamps. Characters: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot. The scene should depict Polly granting access or approving something on a glowing retro terminal.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny clerk visor, stamping a glowing amber digital ticket at a retro wooden desk. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby, its screen showing a bright green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm amber pastel gradient background. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
